name: Bobby Keys
abstract: Robert Henry Keys (December 18, 1943 – December 2, 2014) was an American saxophonist who performed as a member of several horn sections of the 1970s. He appears on albums by the Rolling Stones, Lynyrd Skynyrd, Harry Nilsson, Delaney & Bonnie and Friends, George Harrison, John Lennon, Ringo Starr, Eric Clapton, Joe Cocker, Joe Ely, and ot…

concerts.label: Delaney & Bonnie and Friends with Billy Preston, Kenny Loggins, and Jim Messina

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: we would kind of — would interface with Bobby in how we were going to — what horn lines we were playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Ries
about_quotes.evidence: "the original song with Bobby Keys I’m not playing like Bobby but I don’t want to be too far from it"
about_quotes.doc_title: Tim Ries interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 19, 2022
